file_insights.purpose: Zod validation schemas for Menu module
key_patterns: Centralized LIMITS constant for all validation boundaries
Base schemas for reusable field validation
Support for optional and nullable fields
Type inference from schemas using z.infer<>
validation_coverage: Menu creation, update, publish, and list operations
Category creation, update, reorder, and list operations
Product creation and update with nested variants/modifiers
Nutrition info, allergens, and pricing validation
Complex transformations (string to number, enum handling)

file_insights.purpose: TypeScript type definitions for Menu module
key_patterns: Comprehensive type definitions aligned with schema
Support for database models and API contracts

patterns_discovered.description: LIMITS object centralizes all validation boundaries (min/max values) making maintenance easier and preventing magic numbers
patterns_discovered.benefit: Single source of truth for validation rules

patterns_discovered.description: Reusable base schemas (nameSchema, slugSchema, priceSchema) are combined to build complex schemas, reducing duplication
TypeScript types are inferred directly from Zod schemas using z.infer<>, ensuring type and validation parity
patterns_discovered.benefit: Single source of truth for types and validation

patterns_discovered.description: Complex nested structures (variants, modifiers, nutrition info) are validated using nested schema objects with array transformations
patterns_discovered.benefit: Comprehensive validation of related data structures

patterns_discovered.description: Schemas handle multiple input formats (string/number for prices, string/boolean for flags) with transformations
patterns_discovered.benefit: Compatible with various input sources (APIs, forms, databases)

patterns_discovered.description: Dedicated query schemas for list operations with pagination, filtering, search, and sorting support

gotchas_discovered.description: Price fields use union types and refine() methods with multiple validation chains for string/number handling and range checking. This is powerful but could be error-prone if limits change.
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Store limits in LIMITS constant; document expected behavior in comments

gotchas_discovered.description: Inconsistent use of .optional() and .nullable() - some fields use both, others use just one. This could confuse developers about the expected behavior.
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Document convention: use .optional() for fields that may not be provided, .nullable() for fields that can be explicitly null

gotchas_discovered.description: slugSchema automatically applies .toLowerCase() transformation, which silently modifies input. Users might not expect this behavior.
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Ensure API documentation clearly states slug normalization behavior

gotchas_discovered.description: Query schemas transform string values to booleans (e.g., 'true' -> true). This only works for exact 'true' string; other values become undefined, which might not be obvious.
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Add validation error message or use explicit enum for clarity

gotchas_discovered.description: menuSettingsSchema and themeSettingsSchema both default to empty objects, but callers might expect all fields to have sensible defaults. Consumers need to handle missing fields.
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Consider merging with database defaults or document expected default behavior

approach_outcome.description: Successfully created comprehensive Menu module structure with complete type definitions and validation schemas
deliverables_completed: menu.schema.ts - 862 lines of Zod validation schemas
approach_outcome.validation_approach: Zod-based schema validation with type inference
schema_coverage: Menu CRUD operations (create, update, publish, list)
Product management (create, update with nested relations)
key_decisions: Used Zod for runtime validation with TypeScript type inference
Centralized validation limits in LIMITS constant
Created reusable base schemas to reduce duplication
Implemented nested schema support for complex data structures
Added flexible input handling for multiple formats (string/number, string/boolean)

recommendations.recommendation: Document the distinction between .optional() and .nullable() fields with clear examples in schema file comments
recommendations.rationale: Developers need to understand the difference between 'field not provided' vs 'field explicitly set to null'

recommendations.recommendation: Create comprehensive test suites for edge cases: price boundaries, slug transformations, nested validations, and query parameter parsing
recommendations.rationale: Complex validation logic with multiple transformations requires thorough testing to prevent regressions

recommendations.recommendation: Create branded types for validated inputs (e.g., ValidatedMenuInput) to distinguish between raw and validated data
recommendations.rationale: Would prevent accidental use of unvalidated data in business logic

recommendations.recommendation: Extract price validation logic into a reusable helper function to reduce duplication between priceSchema and optionalPriceSchema
recommendations.rationale: Current duplication of validation logic increases maintenance burden

recommendations.recommendation: Add explicit error messages for all regex validations (slug, color hex) with examples of valid formats
recommendations.rationale: Users will get clearer guidance on what format is expected

recommendations.recommendation: Consider using .superRefine() for complex validations to provide more granular error messages with field paths
recommendations.rationale: Would improve API error responses for form validation scenarios

recommendations.recommendation: Consider splitting menu.schema.ts into separate files by feature (menu.schema.ts, category.schema.ts, product.schema.ts) once it exceeds 1000 lines
recommendations.rationale: Single 862-line file is manageable but future growth might benefit from organization
